claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by understanding your situation:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex who is 18.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and isn't studying anything right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me ask a few more questions to fully assess your eligibility.

Does Alex live with you, and are you responsible for him (meaning you provide for his care and support)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home and I'm responsible for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as another parent or guardian?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no one else claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me and the UK government's Child Benefit rules, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why: While you meet most of the requirements (you live in the UK, Alex lives with you, you're responsible for him, and no one else is claiming), the critical issue is Alex's age and education status.

Child Benefit normally covers children under 16. For young people aged 16 or over, Child Benefit can continue **only if** they are under 20 **and** staying in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any education or training, he doesn't meet this requirement.

**Important to note:** If Alex were to start approved education or training in the future while still under 20, you could potentially become eligible again. This includes things like A-levels, certain vocational qualifications, or approved training courses.

If you have any other questions or if Alex's circumstances change, please don't hesitate to reach out.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 but not in any education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. While you live in the UK, Alex lives with you, you are responsible for him, and no one else is claiming for him - all of which would otherwise support eligibility - the key determining factor is that he has reached 18 and is not continuing in approved education or training.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex and is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. However, Alex is not currently in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility continues past age 16 only if the child is under 20 and remains in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and not in education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

However, Alex is not currently in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, eligibility continues past age 16 only if the child is under 20 and remains in approved education or training. Since Alex is 18 and not in education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
